Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets analyze the temporal profile again to make sure I cover the key anomalies 1 R P 90000 3600 25 hours avg 1 hour stdev Very tight distribution Suspicious rigidity Almost like scheduled batch approval 2 P N 604800 172800 7 days avg 2 days stdev Very long delay Backlog Manual notification process 3 A C 7200 3600 2 hours avg 1 hour stdev Very fast close after assign Missing intermediate steps E P Premature closure 4 E N 300 60 5 minutes avg Too fast Automated or skipping steps P

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt specifically highlights R P P N A C E N Ill include those and perhaps E C

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For hypotheses I need to make them concrete R P low variance Automated approval job running on fixed schedule or bulk approval process at specific hour daily Systemic artificial timestamping e g nightly batch marks all pending as approved P N high mean variance Customer notification is manual and queued or notification sent by external postal service vs email causing bimodality or resource constraint in customer communication team A C short Straight-through processing for low-value claims or forced closure to meet KPIs without proper evaluation or data quality issue duplicate close events E N short Automated notification triggered immediately upon evaluation completion bypassing mandatory approval for certain claim types or system auto-generating notifications before formal approval

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For SQL queries I need PostgreSQL compatible queries Schema claim_events has claim_id activity timestamp resource additional_info claims has claim_type customer_id etc adjusters has adjuster_id region etc Query ideas 1 Find claims where R P is outside expected range e g Z-score 3 or simply outside avg N stdev Join events to themselves 2 Correlate with adjusters claim types resources 3 Filter claims closed immediately after assignment or claims where approval to notification takes excessively long checking if these patterns align with particular customer or region segments

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: use MIN timestamp FILTER WHERE activity R as r_time MIN timestamp FILTER WHERE activity P as p_time WHERE ABS EXTRACT EPOCH FROM p_time - r_time - 90000 3 * 3600

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RP rigidity Nightly batch job auto-approves claims timestamps are system-generated rather than event-driven service-level agreement enforced by automated script at fixed 24-hour cadence PN delay Customer notifications batched weekly third-party mailing service introduces lag manual review queue for notification step staffed intermittently AC speed Low-value claims auto-closed via straight-through processing without evaluation adjusters force-closing claims to meet volume targets erroneous double-entry of close events immediately after assignment EN speed Notification triggered automatically by evaluation system before formal sign-off certain claim types e g rejected claims auto-notify without approval clock synchronization errors between servers

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Double check SQL syntax for PostgreSQL MIN FILTER WHERE is valid EXTRACT EPOCH FROM timestamp1 - timestamp2 returns seconds in PostgreSQL Yes LEFT JOIN adjusters a ON CAST ca resource AS INTEGER = a adjuster_id is valid assuming clean data

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Need to be careful If resource in claim_events refers to adjuster_id for activity E or A we can join But in the period between A and C there might be multiple resources Lets query based on the adjuster assigned in A or the resource in E Lets assume resource during A or E links to adjusters adjuster_id maybe as integer or string Query sketch for adjuster correlation on P N delay
